title: typegen TS
manager: `typegen` — generate TypeScript type definitions from a machine, so a TypeScript host gets compile-time-checked state names, action names, and data shapes instead of stringly-typed guesses. This is the feature that makes FSL pleasant to consume from TS: your editor autocompletes the machine's states and the compiler catches a typo'd transition name before it runs. TypeScript-first now; the fleet-wide typegen across many languages is era 6.
cs: `typegen` TypeScript emitter (§16); states/actions/data-shape types; ungated (no verification dependency); the fleet-wide typegen is era 6.
